trohde: TBH, I’m bad at counting
trohde: have to take off my shoes to count to 20
trohde: also, not sure about my rank
trohde: EGF is 13k … here I dropped from 13 to 15 after I lost a few games on time … so perhaps the handicap was one or two stones too many …
trohde: but what I meant about my bad counting: I never—well: seldom—know,  for sure whether I have won or lost a game until it’s done
trohde: so I also often also don’t know when it’s time to play safe move to endgame or try to complicate things
trohde: and score estimation here seems far from solid
trohde: sometimes I’ve asked stronger opponents to tell me if it was time for me to resign, that I wouldn’t want to be impolite by playing a lost game on and on :-D
trohde: but here the B central territory looks quite good, OTOH upper left and center looks blacker than it is <shrug>

C[Polama: =). Counting is actually alot easier than it seems. Counting by 2's helps, you say ""1"" for a captured stone and otherwise pair empty spots off. I usually go group by group in a circle to avoid missing/double counting. I count up one player, and then subtract the other player so I don't have to remember two numbers at once.
Polama: I can't say it's a huge part of how I play, I usually don't count at all in most real-time games. But dan players always seem to think it's an important skill
C[trohde: aargh … guess I’ll stick with estimating and gut feeling for now … :-D
